A gas engine working on the Otto cycle has a cylinder of diameter 250 mm, stroke 280 mm and clearance volume of 1950 cc. Assume specific heat at constant volume as 0.717 kJ/kgK and specific heat at constant pressure as 1.004 kJ/kgK, the air-standard efficiency of the cycle is _______ %.
(Round off to two decimal places)

NAT
Correct answer: 56.58 (56.00 - 57.10)
Solution

56.58 (56.00 - 57.10)

Consider an adiabatic flow of a perfect gas ( = 1.3 and R = 0.469 kJ/kgK) through a duct. The pressure, temperature and Mach number at the entry are 2.55 bar, 26.5°C and 1.5 respectively. The stagnation temperature at the entry is _______ kN. (Round off to 2 decimal places)

NAT
Correct answer: 400.58 (398.00 - 402.00)
Solution

400.58 (398.00 - 402.00)

Q9 Not checked
Technical • MECHANICAL ENGINEERING • +1 / -0
Not attempted

In the context of compressible flow, which of the following statement(s) is/are CORRECT?

Correct answer: N/A
Solution

(a, b, d)
• The stagnation temperature remains constant in an adiabatic flow because there is no heat transfer.
• The stagnation pressure decreases across a normal shock wave due to irreversible losses.
• The Mach number downstream of a normal shock is less than 1.
• The specific entropy increases across a normal shock wave due to the irreversibility of the shock wave, indicating an increase in disorder.

Correct answer: D
Solution

(d)

Correct answer: B

Correct answer: N/A

(a, d)
